text.title: Agent Integration for Figma AI Ecosystem
text.summary: Practical agent workflows: prompt preparation, webhook-triggered audits, Figma Sites post-publish validation, and key gotchas.

rule.statement: Agents prepare structured Figma Make prompts as versioned documents and log them for human execution.
rule.rationale: Figma Make has no versioning; re-generating with the same prompt produces different results. Logging prepared prompts makes iteration traceable and the prompt library improvable over time.

rule.statement: After a Figma Sites publish, always run a Lighthouse + axe audit — Figma does not validate accessibility or performance on publish.
rule.rationale: Figma Sites published output is not accessibility-validated. Published pages may fail WCAG contrast, missing ARIA labels, and performance requirements that a designer cannot detect in the Figma canvas.

rule.statement: Separate Figma AI output frames from production design frames using a naming convention (e.g., "[AI Draft] Feature Name").
rule.rationale: Figma Make output is non-deterministic and prototype-grade. Without a clear naming separator, AI draft frames may be accidentally included in developer handoff or treated as canonical designs.

list.item: Figma REST API (SaaS) — read file structure, components, styles; limited write; no AI feature access
Figma Webhooks API (SaaS) — event-driven: file_update, library_publish, comment; trigger agent audits
Figma Make (SaaS, UI-only) — prompt-to-prototype; no API
Figma Sites (SaaS, UI-only for publish) — published URLs auditable by agents post-publish
Lighthouse CLI — audit Figma Sites pages for performance and accessibility
axe-cli — WCAG accessibility audit on published Figma Sites pages
Playwright — E2E interaction test on Figma Sites pages
Builder.io (SaaS) — Figma plugin + REST API; converts Figma designs to production-quality code

antipattern.description: Diffing a Figma file from a webhook event without scoping to changed node subtrees.
antipattern.reason: Figma Webhooks send file-level events, not node-level. An agent cannot know which specific frame changed without diffing the full file before and after — which is expensive for large files. Scope webhook reactions to specific frame ranges.

antipattern.description: Working on original assets with Figma AI Image Tools instead of copies.
antipattern.reason: Figma AI image edits (erase, isolate, expand) are not reversible after file save. Always work on copies of assets. Confirm with the designer before applying destructive image operations.

text.reference: Structured Figma Make prompt template for feature prototypes
Lighthouse + axe audit for Figma Sites published output
